name: Ultra-rare kaon decays K -> pi nu nu
requirement: Any candidate framework must reproduce the branching ratios for the 'golden modes' K+ -> pi+ nu nu and K_L -> pi0 nu nu. NA62 measures Br(K+ -> pi+ nu nu) = (1.06 +0.40 -0.34) x 10^-10, in agreement with SM prediction (8.4 +/- 1.0) x 10^-11. KOTO has set Br(K_L -> pi0 nu nu) < 4.1 x 10^-9.

regime.description: Neutral and charged kaon decays; cleanest theoretical SM predictions in flavor physics due to short-distance dominance and known form factors.

current_bound.units: branching ratio (90% CL upper bound for K_L)
current_bound.reference: NA62 Collaboration, 'Measurement of the very rare K+ -> pi+ nu nu decay', JHEP 06, 093 (2021), arXiv:2103.15389; KOTO Collaboration, PRL 122, 021802 (2019), arXiv:1810.09655
current_bound.confidence_level: NA62: 1 sigma; KOTO: 90% CL upper limit

what_it_excludes: These modes are theoretically the cleanest probes of flavor-changing neutral currents in the kaon sector. Constrains Z-prime, leptoquark, and SUSY models with CP-violating squark mixing. A KOTO measurement of Br(K_L -> pi0 nu nu) > 5 x 10^-10 would be a clean smoking gun for new physics; KOTO is currently approaching SM-relevant sensitivity.

parameterization.name: Weak effective theory: Wilson coefficient C_nu of the operator (s_bar gamma^mu d) (nu_bar gamma_mu nu).
parameterization.reference: Buchalla, Buras, NPB 412, 106 (1994); Buras, Gambino, Haisch, NPB 570, 117 (2000).
parameterization.summary: Branching ratios depend on |C_nu^SM + C_nu^NP|^2; NP can shift either or generate right-handed contributions.

procedural.predicted_observable: Br(K+ -> pi+ nu nu) = kappa_+ * |C_nu_SM + C_nu_NP|^2 with kappa_+ a calculable kinematic factor.
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: |C_nu_NP / C_nu_SM| < ~0.5 currently; KOTO bound much weaker on K_L.
framework_inputs: Wilson coefficient of (s_bar gamma^mu d) (nu_bar gamma_mu nu) at scale m_c, with both left- and right-handed quark currents.

primary_references.citation: NA62 Collaboration, 'Measurement of the very rare K+ -> pi+ nu nu decay', JHEP 06, 093 (2021)
KOTO Collaboration, 'Search for the K_L -> pi0 nu nu and K_L -> pi0 X^0 decays at the J-PARC KOTO experiment', PRL 122, 021802 (2019)
Buras, Buttazzo, Knegjens, JHEP 11, 166 (2015), 'K+ -> pi+ nu nu and K_L -> pi0 nu nu in the SM: status and perspectives'
